system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

numberOfSubdomains  4;

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvConstraints;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

mixture

internalField   uniform 300;

boundaryField

    }

    outlet

        inletValue      uniform 300;

    }

    innerWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    outerWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Inlet section (x = -50)
    (-50  25  25)   // 0
    (-50  25 -25)   // 1
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 2
    (-50 -25  25)   // 3

    // Transition/bend section (x = 0 to x = 25)
    (  0  25  25)   // 4
    (  0  25 -25)   // 5
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 6
    (  0 -25  25)   // 7

    ( 25   0  25)   // 8
    ( 25   0 -25)   // 9
    ( 25 -50 -25)   // 10
    ( 25 -50  25)   // 11

    // Outlet section (x = 25 to x = -500)
    (-500 -75  25)  // 12
    (-500 -75 -25)  // 13
    (-500 -125 -25) // 14
    (-500 -125  25) // 15
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: inlet straight duct
    // Vertices: 0,3,2,1 (bottom) and 4,7,6,5 (top)
    // x: -50 -> 0, y: -25 -> 25, z: -25 -> 25
    hex (4 7 6 5 0 3 2 1)        (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: bend section
    // Vertices: 4,7,6,5 (bottom) and 8,11,10,9 (top)
    // x: 0 -> 25, y: -25 -> 0, z: -25 -> 25
    hex (8 11 10 9 4 7 6 5)      (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: outlet straight duct
    // Vertices: 8,11,10,9 (bottom) and 12,15,14,13 (top)
    // x: 25 -> -500, y: -75 -> -25, z: -25 -> 25
    hex (12 15 14 13 8 11 10 9)  (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (12 13 14 15)
        );
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 5 1)
            (4 8 9 5)
            (8 12 13 9)
        );
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
            (7 11 10 6)
            (11 15 14 10)
        );
    }
    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4)
            (4 7 11 8)
            (8 11 15 12)
        );
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 5 6 2)
            (5 9 10 6)
            (9 13 14 10)
        );
    }
    frontWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
            (4 5 9 8)
            (8 9 13 12)
        );
    }
    backWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 2 6 7)
            (7 6 10 11)
            (11 10 14 15)
        );
    }
);

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

There are two persistent errors:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: `Block hex (4 7 6 5 0 3 2 1) is inside-out`
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cascading error (cannot find mesh, caused by blockMesh failure)

## Error 1: Block 0 is Inside-Out

**Block 0:** `hex (4 7 6 5 0 3 2 1)`

Let me trace through the vertices:
- Vertex 0: (-50, 25, 25)
- Vertex 1: (-50, 25, -25)
- Vertex 2: (-50, -25, -25)
- Vertex 3: (-50, -25, 25)
- Vertex 4: (0, 25, 25)
- Vertex 5: (0, 25, -25)
- Vertex 6: (0, -25, -25)
- Vertex 7: (0, -25, 25)

Bottom face: `(4 7 6 5)` — vertices at x=0: (0,25,25)→(0,-25,25)→(0,-25,-25)→(0,25,-25)
Top face: `(0 3 2 1)` — vertices at x=-50: (-50,25,25)→(-50,-25,25)→(-50,-25,-25)→(-50,25,-25)

Viewing the bottom face from outside the block (from x > 0 looking toward x = 0), the interior of the block is in the **-x direction**. Walking 4→7→6→5: (0,25,25)→(0,-25,25)→(0,-25,-25)→(0,25,-25), the interior (-x) is indeed on the **left**. ✅

Viewing the top face from outside (from x < -50 looking toward x = -50), the interior (+x direction) should be on the left. Walking 0→3→2→1: (-50,25,25)→(-50,-25,25)→(-50,-25,-25)→(-50,25,-25), the interior (+x) is on the **right** ❌.

The top face winding is wrong. To fix this, swap the top face vertices so the interior (+x) moves to the left:

Change block 0 from:
```
hex (4 7 6 5 0 3 2 1)
```
to:
```
hex (4 7 6 5 1 2 3 0)
```

## Error 2: Block 1 Connectivity Issue

After fixing block 0, block 1 is `hex (8 11 10 9 4 7 6 5)`. This block connects x=25 to x=0. The bottom face `(8 11 10 9)` is at x=25 and the top face `(4 7 6 5)` is at x=0. Using the same logic as above, the top face `(4 7 6 5)` has the wrong winding (interior should be on the left when viewed from x > 0, but it's on the right). Change block 1 to:

```
hex (8 11 10 9 5 6 7 4)
```

## Error 3: Block 2 Connectivity Issue

Block 2 is `hex (12 15 14 13 8 11 10 9)`. This connects x=-500 to x=25. The bottom face `(12 15 14 13)` is at x=-500 and the top face `(8 11 10 9)` is at x=25. The bottom face `(12 15 14 13)` viewed from x < -500 looks toward increasing x; the interior (+x) should be on the left. Walking 12→15→14→13: (-500,-75,25)→(-500,-125,25)→(-500,-125,-25)→(-500,-75,-25), the interior (+x) is on the **right**. Wrong winding. Change block 2 to:

```
hex (12 15 14 13 9 10 11 8)
```

## Summary of Changes

In `system/blockMeshDict`, replace the three block definitions with:

```
hex (4 7 6 5 1 2 3 0)      (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
hex (8 11 10 9 5 6 7 4)    (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
hex (12 15 14 13 9 10 11 8)(10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
```

Each block's first face is at the more negative x-location and the second face at the more positive x-location, with vertex orderings adjusted so that the interior of the block lies on the left when traversing each face.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
